steps.title: Use the definition
steps.content: We want $f(1) = k$ to appear directly as the coefficient or base. Plug $x=1$ into each form and check which form makes the substitution trivial — i.e., the exponent becomes $0$.

steps.title: Test the choices
steps.content:
For C, $f(x) = 144.5(1.7)^{x-1}$: at $x=1$, exponent $= 0$, so $f(1) = 144.5(1.7)^0 = 144.5 \cdot 1 = 144.5$. The coefficient $144.5$ is $k = f(1)$.

A gives $f(1)=50(1.7)^2$ — coefficient isn't $f(1)$. B gives $f(1)=85(1.7)$. D gives $f(1)=245.65(1.7)^{-1}$. Only C exposes $k$ as the coefficient.

That confirms C is correct.
